previous_followup.verification_method_for_this_run: Each finding below cites concrete on-disk evidence: file paths, line counts, dry-run output captured from this session's bash. No previous-finding STILL_BROKEN/FIXED reclassification is in scope for a directive-override run.

findings.evidence: monitor/scripts/verify-pending-state.js — 290 lines, version 1.0.0, --dry-run flag present. Contract per DIRECTIVE-20260502-003.deliverable_requested.file_1_verify_pending_state. Iterates entries in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json (issues[]) and monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (items[]) whose status matches /-pending-verification$/. Mech 1a path: runs verification_pattern as shell command (typically 'git show origin/main:<patch_file> | grep -q <patched_string>'); pass = exit 0. Mech 1b path: status='resolved-pending-verification' AND verification_artifact_path present → test -f against working tree. Terminal status mapping: fixed-pending-verification → fixed; resolved-pending-verification → resolved; integrated-pending-verification → integrated. On pass: writes verified_at + verified_by_run + verifier_script_version. On fail: writes last_verify_attempt + last_verify_attempt_failure_reason. Defensive default: malformed/missing verification_pattern leaves entry pending and logs error. Idempotency: terminal entries skipped, never-pending entries skipped, re-run = no-op. Per-entry failures NEVER cause non-zero exit. Output report at monitor/integrity/verify-pending-run-<ISO>.json.
findings.self_test_dry_run_output: verify-pending-state.js v1.0.0 (DRY RUN): checked=0, flipped=0, still_pending=0, errors=0
findings.self_test_synthetic_fixture: Tested against an in-memory 8-entry fixture covering all paths: already-terminal (skip), shell-pass (flip), shell-fail (still_pending), missing-pattern (error), file-exists Mech 1b (flip), file-missing Mech 1b (still_pending), tracker-style integrated path (flip), unknown-suffix (error). Result: checked=7, flipped=3, still_pending=2, errors=2. All paths exercised correctly; defensive default verified (errored entries left pending, never flipped).
findings.spec_deviations: None. Implementation follows DIRECTIVE-20260502-003.deliverable_requested.file_1_verify_pending_state contract literally. The verification_pattern is treated as a shell command (per PROP-014 sub_mechanism_1a_push_verify_decider.diff_sketch which gives the verification_pattern as a literal command string). The script runs from the workspace-sync clone where `git pull --rebase` has just synced HEAD with origin/main, so test -f against the working tree IS testing origin/main.
findings.operator_action_required: 1) Review the script (header docstring + implementation). 2) Run `node monitor/scripts/verify-pending-state.js --dry-run` from a clone to confirm zero-flip baseline. 3) Authorize for workspace-sync invocation by leaving the workspace-sync.md Step 3.6 edit in place (already done in F3 below).

findings.summary: Authored monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js (PROP-014 Mech 3 paragraph-citation audit, Stage 1 + Stage 2)
findings.evidence: monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js — 448 lines, version 1.0.0, --dry-run + --since=YYYY-MM-DD + --all flags. Contract per DIRECTIVE-20260502-003.deliverable_requested.file_2_audit_narrative_citations. Audited surfaces (configurable list of (file_glob, json_path) at top of script): daily-report-*.json (pipeline_status.{poller,analyst,curmudgeon,decider} + recommended_actions[].action), curmudgeon/reviews/*.json (kernel_of_truth.description, kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim, our_argument_summary), tinker/report-*.json (findings[].description). Stage 1 paragraph gate: every paragraph >1 sentence MUST contain at least one citation matching /\(([^)]+\.(json|jsonl|txt|md))(:[\w-]+)?\)/. Stage 2 anchor match: for citations with anchor (':<field>'), test -f cited file; for JSON, recursive key search; for .md/.txt, literal substring match. Edge cases: empty prose skipped, triple-backtick code blocks stripped, single-backtick inline code stripped, headers/list-items excluded from paragraph extraction, single-sentence paragraphs not audited. Idempotency: purely observational, no input mutation. Output report at monitor/integrity/narrative-cite-audit-<ISO>.json.
findings.self_test_dry_run_output: audit-narrative-citations.js v1.0.0 (DRY RUN, --since=2026-05-02): files=10, paragraphs=17, uncited=17 (100.0%), citations=0, bogus_anchors=0 (0.0% of anchored), file_missing=0. Stage 1: 17 uncited paragraph(s) above 1-sentence threshold. THIS IS THE EXPECTED PRE-ROLLOUT BASELINE — all existing daily-reports / reviews predate the Mech 3 narrative-cite discipline that operator landed in this morning's prompt edits. The 14-day rolling window will incorporate post-rollout files as they accrue, and the uncited percentage should fall toward 0% as Mech-3-disciplined daily-reports / reviews ship.
findings.self_test_synthetic_fixture: Tested with a synthetic daily-report fixture containing 3 multi-sentence paragraphs (one uncited, two with citations + bogus anchors targeting non-existent JSON keys). Result: paragraphs_total=3, paragraphs_uncited=1 (correctly identified the uncited one), citations_total=4, citations_with_anchor=4, citations_bogus_anchor=4 (correctly flagged all four anchors as 'not found in JSON tree'), file_missing=0 (real files cited, just bogus anchors). Single-sentence recommended_action correctly excluded by sentence-count gate. Sample arrays populated correctly with first-3 hits and per-surface_field labels.
findings.spec_deviations: Two minor deviations from DIRECTIVE-20260502-003.deliverable_requested.file_2_audit_narrative_citations, both flagged in script header docstring: (a) Default scan window narrowed from 'all' to 'last 14 days' (matches PROP-014-amendment-001 Q3 acceptance window); the --all flag overrides to scan every file, --since=YYYY-MM-DD narrows arbitrarily. Reason: pre-rollout files are guaranteed to fail Stage 1 and would drown the soft-complaint signal. (b) Line count is 448 vs spec estimate of ~150. Reason: explicit per-edge-case handling (code blocks, list items, headers, sample collection, JSON walk) plus configurable surface table (4 surfaces, 1-line-edit to add more). The directive says '~150 lines' as estimate, not contract; functionality fits the contract literally.
findings.operator_action_required: 1) Review the script (header docstring + implementation). 2) Run `node monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js --dry-run --since=2026-05-02` to see today's baseline (expected: high uncited % because pre-rollout). 3) On authorization, workspace-sync's Step 3.6 invocation (F3 below) will run the audit hourly and the soft-complaint signal will surface in run reports.

findings.summary: Modified monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md to invoke both scripts at Step 3.6
findings.evidence: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 3.6 inserted between existing Step 3.5 (NEVER_PUSH strand rescue) and Step 4 (commit + push). ~85 lines added. Contract per DIRECTIVE-20260502-003.deliverable_requested.file_3_workspace_sync_modification: (1) Runs `node monitor/scripts/verify-pending-state.js` against freshly-pulled clone state — yes, verifier runs after Step 1's git pull --rebase. (2) Runs `node monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js` against same — yes. (3) If verify-pending-state.js made flips, those files get re-staged so they push back to git — yes, via `git add monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json monitor/integrity/`. (4) If audit-narrative-citations.js found uncited paragraphs, write audit report to monitor/integrity/ AND append soft-complaint line to workspace-sync run report — yes, the audit script writes the report itself; Step 3.6 instructs the agent to include 'soft-complaint: narrative-cite uncited=N bogus=N' literal in Step 4b's AGENT_NOTES so tinker's existing soft-complaints grep catches it. Backward compatibility: both invocations are guarded by `[ -f monitor/scripts/<script>.js ]` so an old clone without the scripts gracefully skips the step rather than erroring.
findings.spec_deviations: None of substance. The directive said 'Step 3.6 or wherever fits best'; chose 3.6 between existing 3.5 and 4. The directive said the soft-complaint line should append to the workspace-sync run report (Step 4b); rather than re-architect Step 4b's run-report-writing block, instructed the LLM agent in Step 3.6 to include the literal 'soft-complaint' string in Step 4b's AGENT_NOTES if Mech 3 surfaces uncited or bogus signals. This piggybacks on the existing AGENT_NOTES grep target and is the minimum-surface change.
findings.operator_action_required: Review the diff between current workspace-sync.md and the inserted Step 3.6 block. Confirm the soft-complaint guidance reads correctly. Once authorized, the next workspace-sync run (~hourly) will pick up the new step and invoke both scripts.

findings.summary: Self-test fixture file orphaned in monitor/decisions/ due to FUSE no-unlink constraint
findings.evidence: During F2 self-test, a synthetic daily-report-9999-99-99T99-99.json fixture was written to monitor/decisions/ to validate the audit script's Stage 1 + Stage 2 logic. The FUSE workspace mount cannot `unlink()`, so post-test cleanup `rm` failed with 'Operation not permitted' (this is the documented constraint cited in CLAUDE.md Two-Repo Architecture section). Mitigation applied: file overwritten with audit-invisible content (no pipeline_status / recommended_actions / findings keys, plus _note explaining provenance and _can_be_removed:true marker). Subsequent dry-run audits confirm the fixture no longer trips Stage 1 (paragraph counts dropped from 20 to 17 post-overwrite).
findings.operator_action_required: When convenient, `git rm monitor/decisions/daily-report-9999-99-99T99-99.json` from a clean clone, commit, and push. The file is harmless until removed (audit-invisible) but adds 1 stale entry to monitor/decisions/ listings.

recommended_next_operator_actions: 1. Review the three deliverables (scripts + workspace-sync.md diff). Run --dry-run on each script in your local clone to confirm the output shape.
2. If satisfied, commit and push the three files. The next workspace-sync run (within ~1h) will start invoking both scripts.
3. Watch the first 2-3 workspace-sync run reports for Mech 3 soft-complaint signals in AGENT_NOTES. The pre-rollout uncited% should drop as Mech-3-disciplined daily-reports ship.

4. Watch for the first decider self-apply that writes status='fixed-pending-verification' — that's Mech 1's first real exercise. Expected: workspace-sync's next cycle picks it up, verification passes against origin/main, status flips to 'fixed', verified_at + verified_by_run + verifier_script_version land in the same commit.
5. Optional: `git rm` the orphan test fixture monitor/decisions/daily-report-9999-99-99T99-99.json (F5). Audit-invisible but visible in directory listings.
6. Defer next priority pending directive (DIRECTIVE-20260427-001-workspace-sync-comment-refactor.json, low priority) to a future tinker run.
